text: Gonadotropins are hormones in mammals that cue development of reproductive organs to maturity (Casarini and Simoni 2021; Howard 2021) and the different phases of the estrus cycle (Uenoyama et al. 2021).  Gonadotropins are composed of two subunits: a 90-100 amino acid alpha subunit that is identical for all gonadotropins for a species, and a 105-150 amino acid beta subunit that are unique to each gonadotropin but exhibit large similarities in order to interact with alpha subunits (Cahoreau et al 2015).  Key gonadotropins include Follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) and Luteinizing hormone (LH), which are released from the anterior pituitary gland (Howard 2021).
